utility_details.judge_error: In the wo_skills run, the agent made a critical calculation error: it only counted post-January 2008 mobilization periods for the § 12731b early retirement credit (omitting the pre-2008 OIF 2003-2004 and Katrina 2005 periods, which is technically correct under the law), but then miscounted the number of qualifying 90-day blocks within each post-2008 period. Specifically, for COVID 2020-2021 it counted 5 blocks (456 days) instead of the correct 4 or fewer, and similarly overcounted other periods, arriving at 39 months reduction total instead of the correct 21 months. This produced an RPED of March 7, 2039 instead of September 7, 2040. Additionally, the base pay used for the retirement calculation (~$5,789/month High-3 base) appears to have been applied with an incorrect multiplier or the wrong base, yielding ~$1,947/month instead of ~$5,322/month.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is significantly more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills run invoked the guard-admin skill which provided the correct RPED (2040/09/07), the exact point count (4,843), the correct 21-month early retirement credit calculation, the $5,322/month pay estimate, the $9,676/month CRDP combined figure, and the NGB 23A audit requirement — all key specifics. The wo_skills run, without the skill, attempted independent calculations but made critical errors: the RPED was computed as March 2039 (incorrect), the retirement pay estimate was ~$1,947–$2,016/month (far below the correct ~$5,322), the combined CRDP figure was ~$5,685 (instead of ~$9,676), and the NGB 23A was not specifically flagged. The wo_skills document is structurally competent and passes 4 of 7 evaluation items, but contains material financial inaccuracies that would render the client brief unreliable for actual retirement planning. The wi_skills document passes all 7 evaluation items with accurate, actionable content.
